item.title: word_count
item.description: Vault Stats This vault currently contains 16,716 words across all notes.

item.title: Open Guard General
item.description: When in Gi the Sleeve and collar grip is crazy strong and can even be enhanced by having a leg on opponents waist.

item.title: Concept of ankle control
item.description: A general concept that can be applied to most passing techniques is that by grabbing opponents ankle with cross side arm you can control opponent and limit their movement greatly.

item.title: Armbar
item.description: General Useful and basic finisher which is based on extending the opponents are straight and pressuring the Elbow.

item.title: Turtle
item.description: Lessons learned from attacker side: Pressure Turtle defence Variant of turtle is Panda Starting positions Rear Side Reverse (Head) Attack the neck In Gi start by attacking the neck with Cross collar -grip, in order to pressure opponent to do something.

item.title: Gameplan
item.description: General Goal of this page is to outline a plan of what to do in different scenarios.

item.title: Toreando
item.description: Near side hand on opponents leg and farside on their waist.

item.title: Elevate posture prior to sweeps
item.description: In order to enable any sweep to work you need to NOT have the opponent sitting on their ass.

item.title: Timing
item.description: General This is essentially just a made up concept in order to better explain the timing of Sweeps in the scope of Technique circle, particularly the east/west.
